title: Enterprise Search Decision Memo
brief: A concise executive memo recommending a focused rollout path for enterprise search improvements.

slots.title: Ship enterprise search as a focused workflow layer
slots.lead: The strongest path is a narrow launch around source-backed answers, governed connectors, and measurable support deflection rather than a broad knowledge portal.

slots.heading: Recommendation
slots.recommendation: Approve a 90-day launch focused on three governed workflows: account research, policy lookup, and support answer drafting.
slots.ask: Confirm launch scope and two accountable metrics.
slots.rationale: The narrow scope gives teams a visible quality bar while avoiding a long platform build before users see value.

slots.heading: Why this matters now
slots.body: Teams already search across CRM notes, support history, and internal policy pages, but answers are inconsistent and hard to verify. The launch should solve the highest-frequency workflows first.
slots.note: The product risk is not discovery; it is trusted synthesis with provenance.

slots.heading: Implementation posture
slots.intro: Keep the first release small enough that quality, ownership, and measurement stay legible.

items.label: Constrain the workflow set
items.body: Launch three repeatable workflows with known source systems and user owners.

items.label: Make provenance visible
items.body: Every answer must cite source records, policy pages, or support cases before it can be shared.

items.label: Measure resolution quality
items.body: Track accepted answers, escalation avoidance, and correction reasons from the first pilot week.

slots.heading: Evidence to preserve
slots.intro: The first launch should be judged against observable workflow signals, not broad adoption claims.

rows: Top policy and entitlement questions recur weekly
Start with answer drafting and source citation
Teams copy context across CRM, notes, and tickets
Leaders ask for source visibility before automation

sourceNotes: Synthetic example based on anonymized product discovery patterns.
No customer-specific data is included in this pack example.

slots.heading: Risks and controls
slots.intro: The main risks are quality drift, unclear ownership, and scope expansion before the quality bar is proven.

items.label: Answer trust
items.body: Require citations, correction capture, and human review for low-confidence responses.

items.label: Connector sprawl
items.body: Start with approved systems only, then add connectors when a workflow owner is named.

items.label: Metric ambiguity
items.body: Define accepted answer and avoided escalation before the pilot begins.

slots.heading: Next actions
slots.intro: A narrow launch plan keeps the team aligned while leaving room to expand once quality is proven.

rows: Confirm the three launch workflows and acceptance criteria
Approve source connectors and citation requirements
Run pilot review sessions and capture corrections

slots.heading: Source handling
slots.body: This example demonstrates the document pack contract. Real generated memos should use project-provided sources and explicit notes.

sourceNotes: Pack examples are synthetic and must not be treated as customer evidence.
Runtime source notes should name the uploaded or project-local source used for each claim.
